market_context: US-Iran ceasefire announcement overnight has triggered massive pre-market rally with Dow futures up 2.79%, S&P 500 up 2.45%, and Nasdaq up 3.2%. Oil prices crashed 15%+ on the geopolitical relief, while tech and airline stocks surge in pre-market trading.

predictions.reasoning: Nasdaq futures up 3.2% pre-market on ceasefire news with tech stocks like NVDA, TSLA, AMD surging 4-10%. QQQ after-hours already showing strength at 606.33, indicating strong momentum continuation likely.

NVDA up 3.66% in pre-market to $184.61, riding the tech rally from ceasefire news. Semiconductor stocks mentioned as surging 4-10% pre-market, and NVDA has strong technical support with analyst targets averaging $265.

DAL beat Q1 earnings $0.64 vs $0.61 estimate and revenue beat expectations. Stock already jumped 12.2% pre-market to $73.64. Airline stocks getting double boost from ceasefire (lower oil costs + travel recovery).
WTI crude futures already down 15%+ to $95.75 on ceasefire announcement. Oil supply concerns from Strait of Hormuz closure should continue to unwind as geopolitical tensions ease significantly.

Energy sector should decline as oil prices crash 15%+ on ceasefire news. Oil companies mentioned as declining while broader market rallies. Energy stocks had been war beneficiaries but now face reversal.
